title: 1.1: Tests that recipient display shows only the alias email when alias has no name and is not on a custom domain
1.2: Includes a test that asserts result.name == f"{alias.name} <{alias.email}>" for an alias with alias.name set
1.3: Includes a precedence test (alias.name + custom_domain.name both set) that asserts result.name == f"{alias.name} <{alias.email}>"
1.4: Tests that recipient display shows only email when alias on custom domain but neither have names
1.5: Includes a domain-fallback test (alias.name unset, custom_domain.name set) that asserts result.name == f"{custom_domain.name} <{alias.email}>"
1.6: Tests that no user-facing override indicator is surfaced when no override is applied
1.7: Tests that a user-facing override indicator is surfaced when alias display name override is applied
1.8: Tests that a user-facing override indicator is surfaced when custom domain display name override is applied
2.1: Places the alias recipient name tests in tests/test_alias_utils.py
3.1: All tests use pytest-compatible test functions
3.2: All tests follow the test_<description> naming pattern
3.3: All tests use standard Python assert statements for verification
3.4: Tests create users using established test helpers or fixtures (e.g., create_new_user)
4.1: Tests create persisted Alias objects with required fields for the codepath under test
4.2: Tests involving custom domains create and associate CustomDomain objects with required fields
4.3: Added tests persist ORM objects using commit=True on create calls (e.g., Alias.create(..., commit=True) / CustomDomain.create(..., commit=True))
